userStories.title: RotateKeyDialog i18n migration
userStories.description: Decompose from US-001: migrate RotateKeyDialog hardcoded strings to i18n keys, including reactive copied state labels.
acceptanceCriteria: When RotateKeyDialog renders with a closed state, then the DialogTitle element text is sourced from t('agents.rotateKey.title') not a hardcoded string literal.
When RotateKeyDialog renders the confirmation form, then the paragraph body text is sourced from t('agents.rotateKey.confirmBody') with agent name interpolated, not a hardcoded literal.
When RotateKeyDialog confirmation submit is in-flight (isSubmitting=true), then the submit button label is sourced from t('agents.rotateKey.rotating').
When RotateKeyDialog confirmation submit is idle, then the submit button label is sourced from t('common.confirm').
When RotateKeyDialog cancel button is clicked, then the button label rendered is sourced from t('common.cancel').
When RotateKeyDialog key-reveal section is visible, then the warning paragraph text is sourced from t('agents.rotateKey.apiKeyReveal.message').
When RotateKeyDialog key-reveal copy button is idle, then its label is sourced from t('agents.rotateKey.apiKeyReveal.copy').
When RotateKeyDialog key-reveal copy button has been activated, then its label switches to t('agents.rotateKey.apiKeyReveal.copied').
When RotateKeyDialog key-reveal done button is rendered, then its label is sourced from t('common.done').

userStories.title: Delete/Create dialog i18n migration
userStories.description: Decompose from US-001: migrate DeleteAgentDialog and CreateAgentDialog key-reveal hardcoded strings to i18n keys.
acceptanceCriteria: When DeleteAgentDialog renders, then the DialogTitle element text is sourced from t('agents.deleteAgent.title').
When DeleteAgentDialog renders, then the confirmation body text is sourced from t('agents.deleteAgent.confirm') with agent name interpolated.
When DeleteAgentDialog delete submit is in-flight (isSubmitting=true), then the delete button label is sourced from t('agents.deleteAgent.deleting').
When DeleteAgentDialog delete submit is idle, then the delete button label is sourced from t('agents.actions.delete').
When DeleteAgentDialog cancel button is rendered, then its label is sourced from t('common.cancel').
When CreateAgentDialog key-reveal section is visible, then the warning paragraph text is sourced from t('agents.rotateKey.apiKeyReveal.message').
When CreateAgentDialog key-reveal copy button is idle, then its label is sourced from t('agents.rotateKey.apiKeyReveal.copy').
When CreateAgentDialog key-reveal copy button has been activated, then its label switches to t('agents.rotateKey.apiKeyReveal.copied').
When CreateAgentDialog key-reveal done button is rendered, then its label is sourced from t('common.done').

userStories.title: Locale parity + editCapabilities key fix
userStories.description: Decompose from US-001: ensure en/zh key parity for added keys and fix EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog key mismatch.
acceptanceCriteria: Given apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json contains agents.rotateKey.title, when apps/web/i18n/locales/zh.json is read, then it contains the same key agents.rotateKey.title.
Given apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json contains agents.rotateKey.confirmBody, when apps/web/i18n/locales/zh.json is read, then it contains the same key agents.rotateKey.confirmBody.
Given apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json contains agents.rotateKey.rotating, when apps/web/i18n/locales/zh.json is read, then it contains the same key agents.rotateKey.rotating.
Given apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json contains agents.deleteAgent.title, when apps/web/i18n/locales/zh.json is read, then it contains the same key agents.deleteAgent.title.
Given apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json contains agents.deleteAgent.deleting, when apps/web/i18n/locales/zh.json is read, then it contains the same key agents.deleteAgent.deleting.
Given apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json contains common.done, when apps/web/i18n/locales/zh.json is read, then it contains the same key common.done.
Given apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json contains agents.form.editCapabilities, when apps/web/i18n/locales/zh.json is read, then it contains the same key agents.form.editCapabilities.
When EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog renders, then the DialogTitle element text resolves to a non-empty string (not the raw key agents.form.editCapabilities).

userStories.title: Capabilities input/output contract as string array
userStories.description: Fix the capabilities form field in CreateAgentDialog.vue to store and submit a `string[]` instead of a comma-joined string. Change the zod schema from `z.string().optional()` to `z.array(z.string()).default([])`, remove the hidden `<input>` workaround, update the watcher to call `setFieldValue('capabilities', tags)` with the array, and update `initialValues.capabilities` to `[]`. Fix the zod schema in EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog.vue from `z.string().optional()` to `z.array(z.string()).default([])` and detach `v-bind="componentField"` from the visible tag Input so the form field correctly represents the array. Ensure preloaded agent capabilities initialise the tags array without comma-splitting.
acceptanceCriteria: When CreateAgentDialog onSubmit runs with capabilities tags ['read-code', 'write-tests'], then the body passed to $api.post('/agents') contains capabilities: ['read-code', 'write-tests'] as a string array, not a comma-joined string.
Given CreateAgentDialog has no capability tags added, when onSubmit runs, then the body passed to $api.post('/agents') contains capabilities: [].
When EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog onSubmit runs with capabilitiesTags ['analyze', 'review'], then the body passed to $api.patch contains capabilities: ['analyze', 'review'] as a string array.
Given EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog has no capability tags, when onSubmit runs, then the body passed to $api.patch contains capabilities: [].
Given an agent prop with capabilities: ['foo', 'bar'], when EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog mounts, then capabilitiesTags is initialized to ['foo', 'bar'] without splitting on commas.

Given an agent prop with capabilities: ['multi-word capability'], when EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog mounts, then capabilitiesTags is initialized to ['multi-word capability'] as a single entry, not split into separate entries.

userStories.title: normalizeCapabilities utility behavior
userStories.description: Decompose from US-003: implement and test pure normalizeCapabilities utility (trim, remove empty, case-insensitive dedupe preserving first casing).
acceptanceCriteria: normalizeCapabilities(['Read-Code', 'read-code', 'READ-CODE']) returns a single-element array ['Read-Code'] (preserves first-seen casing, deduplicates case-insensitively).
normalizeCapabilities(['  trim me  ', 'clean']) returns ['trim me', 'clean'] with leading/trailing whitespace removed.
normalizeCapabilities(['valid', '', '   ', 'also-valid']) returns ['valid', 'also-valid'] with empty and whitespace-only entries removed.
normalizeCapabilities([]) returns [].
normalizeCapabilities(['Foo', 'foo', 'FOO', 'bar']) returns ['Foo', 'bar'] (first-seen 'Foo' kept, later case variants dropped).
When addCapability is called in CreateAgentDialog with an input whose trimmed value is empty string, then capabilitiesTags length does not increase.

userStories.title: Capabilities tag UX guards and submit consistency
userStories.description: Decompose from US-003: apply normalization/blank guards in Create/Edit dialogs and verify remove/submit payload consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: When addCapability is called in CreateAgentDialog with a value that matches an existing tag case-insensitively, then capabilitiesTags length does not increase.
When addCapability is called in EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog with an input whose trimmed value is empty string, then capabilitiesTags length does not increase.
When addCapability is called in EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog with a value that matches an existing tag case-insensitively, then capabilitiesTags length does not increase.
When removeCapability is called in CreateAgentDialog with index 1 from tags ['a', 'b', 'c'], then capabilitiesTags becomes ['a', 'c'] and the next onSubmit payload contains capabilities: ['a', 'c'].
When handleRemove is called in EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog with index 0 from tags ['x', 'y'], then capabilitiesTags becomes ['y'] and the next onSubmit payload contains capabilities: ['y'].

analysis:
## Affected Files

### RotateKeyDialog.vue (`apps/web/components/RotateKeyDialog.vue`)
Fully hardcoded UI text throughout: dialog title 'Rotate API Key', confirmation body 'Are you sure you want to rotate the API key for {{ agent.name }}? The old key will become invalid.', button labels 'Cancel'/'Confirm', loading state 'Rotating...', post-rotate key reveal text 'Copy this API key now. It will not be shown again.' and 'Done'. The component already calls `useI18n()` and uses `t('agents.rotateKey.failed')` for the error toast — so the infrastructure is present but template strings were never migrated. `copyButtonText` is a reactive `ref('Copy')` that gets set to `'Copied!'` — this must become a computed flag pattern to allow i18n.

### DeleteAgentDialog.vue (`apps/web/components/DeleteAgentDialog.vue`)
Fully hardcoded template: title 'Delete Agent', confirmation body 'Are you sure you want to delete agent "{{ agent.name }}"? This action cannot be undone.', and buttons 'Cancel'/'Deleting...'/'Delete'. Already has `const { t } = useI18n()` and uses `t('agents.toast.deleted')` / `t('agents.toast.deleteFailed')` in the script but the template was never wired up.

### CreateAgentDialog.vue (`apps/web/components/CreateAgentDialog.vue`)
Partially migrated: the form section uses i18n correctly. The key-reveal section (shown after successful agent creation, lines 111-122) is hardcoded: warning text 'Copy this API key now. It will not be shown again.', done button 'Done'. `copyButtonText` ref is initialized with hardcoded `'Copy'` / `'Copied!'` strings. Additionally the capabilities form field has a structural contract problem: schema uses `z.string().optional()`, the form is initialized with `capabilities: ''` (empty string), and a hidden `<input>` stores `capabilitiesTags.join(',')` as the form value. A `watch` syncs tags to the form field via `tags.join(',')`. The `onSubmit` passes `formValues` directly, so `capabilities` reaches the `POST /agents` body as a comma-joined string instead of `string[]`.

### EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog.vue (`apps/web/components/EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog.vue`)
The dialog title uses `t('agents.form.editCapabilities')` which is a MISSING key in `en.json` (only `agents.editCapabilities.title` exists, creating a key mismatch). The `onSubmit` at line 113 correctly sends `{ capabilities: capabilitiesTags.value }` as an array — so the PATCH payload is already correct. However, the visible `<Input>` has `v-bind="componentField"` binding a `z.string()` typed field directly to the text input, meaning the form validation operates on a string field rather than the array. The normalization (deduplication, trimming) is case-sensitive in `addCapability` — `includes(value)` will allow 'foo' and 'FOO' as separate entries.

### i18n locale files (`apps/web/i18n/locales/en.json`, `zh.json`)
**Already present** keys useful for this feature: `common.cancel`, `common.confirm`, `agents.rotateKey.failed`, `agents.rotateKey.confirm` (but no `{name}` interpolation), `agents.rotateKey.newKey`, `agents.rotateKey.rotate`, `agents.rotateKey.apiKeyReveal.{title,message,copy,copied}`, `agents.deleteAgent.confirm` (has `{name}` interpolation), `agents.deleteAgent.delete`, `agents.actions.delete`.

**Missing keys** that must be added to both locales:
- `common.done` — 'Done' (reused by RotateKeyDialog and CreateAgentDialog)
- `agents.rotateKey.title` — 'Rotate API Key' (dialog title)
- `agents.rotateKey.confirmBody` — 'Are you sure you want to rotate the API key for {name}? The old key will become invalid.' (confirmation with agent name interpolation)
- `agents.rotateKey.rotating` — 'Rotating...' (in-flight button label)
- `agents.deleteAgent.title` — 'Delete Agent' (dialog title)
- `agents.deleteAgent.deleting` — 'Deleting...' (in-flight button label)
- `agents.form.editCapabilities` — 'Edit Capabilities' (fixes existing key mismatch in EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog)

## Architecture & Patterns
- All dialogs use `useI18n()` composable; `t()` is already in scope in all four components.
- For reactive copy button text: replace `ref<string>('Copy')` pattern with a `ref<boolean>(false)` flag (`isCopied`) and render `isCopied ? t('...copied') : t('...copy')` in the template.
- Capabilities contract fix in `CreateAgentDialog`: change schema field to `z.array(z.string()).default([])`, remove hidden input, update watcher to `setFieldValue('capabilities', tags)` (array), and update `initialValues.capabilities` to `[]`.
- `normalizeCapabilities(input: string[]): string[]` should be a pure utility extractable to a shared composable or inline helper — trim each entry, filter empty, deduplicate case-insensitively preserving first-seen casing.
- `EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog` form field binding: detach `v-bind="componentField"` from the visible Input (it should only drive the hidden/array value); the Input is used for tag input, not direct field binding.

## Risks
- The `agents.rotateKey.confirm` key exists but has no `{name}` interpolation — adding `agents.rotateKey.confirmBody` as a new key (rather than mutating the existing one) avoids breaking any consumer that references the old key.
- `copyButtonText` as a string ref is used in both `CreateAgentDialog` and `RotateKeyDialog` — both need the same migration to a boolean `isCopied` flag.
- Changing the `capabilities` form field schema from `z.string()` to `z.array(z.string())` in `CreateAgentDialog` removes the hidden input. Care must be taken that the `setFieldValue` call after tag updates uses the array, not a joined string.
- `EditAgentCapabilitiesDialog` already sends `capabilitiesTags.value` correctly in the PATCH body; the schema change is for correctness/type-safety only and does not break the API call.

## Test Approach
- US-001: Component tests asserting rendered text comes from i18n keys; JSON structure tests asserting key parity between en.json and zh.json.
- US-002: Unit tests on form submission value shape (capabilities field is string[]).
- US-003: Pure unit tests for `normalizeCapabilities`; component-level tests for addCapability UX guards.
